title: Error: container_linux.go:380: starting container process caused: error adding seccomp filter rule for syscall bdflush: permission denied: OCI permission denied
body:
Hi, I am getting this error with latest `podman` my podman info is:
```bash
~ sudo podman info                     
host:
  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.21.0
  cgroupControllers:
  - io
  - memory
  - hugetlb
  - pids
  - rdma
  cgroupManager: systemd
  cgroupVersion: v2
  conmon:
    package: /usr/bin/conmon is owned by conmon 1:2.0.29-1
    path: /usr/bin/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.29, commit: 7e6de6678f6ed8a18661e1d5721b81ccee293b9b'
  cpus: 12
  distribution:
    distribution: arch
    version: unknown
  eventLogger: journald
  hostname: ophion
  idMappings:
    gidmap: null
    uidmap: null
  kernel: 5.10.45-1-lts
  linkmode: dynamic
  memFree: 7517126656
  memTotal: 8102539264
  ociRuntime:
    name: runc
    package: /usr/bin/runc is owned by runc 1.0.0rc95-1
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: |-
      runc version 1.0.0-rc95
      commit: b9ee9c6314599f1b4a7f497e1f1f856fe433d3b7
      spec: 1.0.2-dev
      go: go1.16.4
      libseccomp: 2.5.1
  os: linux
  remoteSocket:
    path: /run/podman/podman.sock
  security:
    apparmorEnabled: false
    capabilities: CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT
    rootless: false
    seccompEnabled: true
    seccompProfilePath: /etc/containers/seccomp.json
    selinuxEnabled: false
  serviceIsRemote: false
  slirp4netns:
    executable: ""
    package: ""
    version: ""
  swapFree: 7999582208
  swapTotal: 7999582208
  uptime: 9m 24.03s
registries:
  search:
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - registry.centos.org
  - docker.io
store:
  configFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf
  containerStore:
    number: 2
    paused: 0
    running: 0
    stopped: 2
  graphDriverName: overlay
  graphOptions:
    overlay.mountopt: nodev
  graphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  graphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "true"
  imageStore:
    number: 15
  runRoot: /run/containers/storage
  volumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes
version:
  APIVersion: 3.2.1
  Built: 1623927305
  BuiltTime: Thu Jun 17 11:55:05 2021
  GitCommit: 152952fe6b18581615c3efd1fafef2d8142738e8
  GoVersion: go1.16.5
  OsArch: linux/amd64
  Version: 3.2.1
  ```
downgrading [containers-common](https://archlinux.org/packages/community/any/containers-common/) from `0.40.0-1` to `0.39.0-3` fixes the issue 

thanks

comments.body:
@cevich Well, we have a report of this in the wild now.

@jxs Workaround for now is to use `crun` instead of `runc` as your OCI runtime. We're seeing this in our CI and are trying to chase down the exact cause.

> Well, we have a report of this in the wild now.

Aye, s'pose it was eventually inevitable.  For reference, this is being [tracked in a containers-common issue](https://github.com/containers/common/issues/631) (but affects both podman and buildah bud).

Thanks @mheon.

`podman --runtime /usr/bin/crun run` worked for me (after installing [crun](https://github.com/containers/crun))
You can change this by default in /etc/containers/containers.conf

Arch maintainer here. Feel free to poke me to test patches and/or if we should add some workarounds.

Are we better off swapping runc for crun in our `podman` package going forward?

That is what we are doing in Fedora land.  We think in the long run having a lighter weight OCI Runtime is a good thing.  Also we hope to get newer "plugin" runtimes that use crun as a front end in the future. libkrun for example.

Just saw this on ArchLinux
```
docker build -t docker.io/haikuporter/haikuporter:1.2.5 .
STEP 1: FROM ubuntu:bionic
STEP 2: RUN ...
level=error msg="container_linux.go:380: starting container process caused: error adding seccomp filter rule for syscall bdflush: permission denied"
: exit status 1
Error: error building at STEP "RUN ...": error while running runtime: exit status 1
```

podman version 3.2.1
switching to crun fixes it.  To have a workaround documented in this ticket, how do you change the default runtime of podman to crun?

Closing this as the [root cause issue](https://github.com/containers/common/issues/631) has also been closed.  To be clear, the fix is podman 2.3.1 (or later) plus either:

* Use crun
* Use runc 1.0.0 (released 23rd June)

> Closing this as the [root cause issue](https://github.com/containers/common/issues/631) has also been closed. To be clear, the fix is podman 2.3.1 (or later) plus either:
> 
> * Use crun
> * Use runc 1.0.0 (released 23rd June)

I run podman 3.3.1 on Gentoo, and with runc 1.0.0 I stumbled on this same error. I installed crun 0.19.1 (latest in Gentoo), and the problem "went away".

> Thanks @mheon.
> 
> `podman --runtime /usr/bin/crun run` worked for me (after installing [crun](https://github.com/containers/crun))

@captn3m0 I can this on Fedora 35:

```
sudo podman --runtime /usr/bin/crun run
Error: requires at least 1 arg(s), only received 0
```

What typo have I done?
You have to specify an image.
Ah, I'm trying to build one from a Dockerfile.

I use the lastst version of crun and podman, but i still raise errors.

[lch@n131-251-011 ~]$ podman run --userns=/usr/bin/runc --userns=keep-id -it docker.io/library/ubuntu:14.04 bash
Error: executable file `bash` not found in $PATH: No such file or directory: OCI runtime attempted to invoke a command that was not found
[lch@n131-251-011 ~]$ podman run --runtime /usr/local/bin/crun --userns=keep-id -it docker.io/library/ubuntu:14.04 bash
Error: executable file `bash` not found in $PATH: No such file or directory: OCI runtime attempted to invoke a command that was not found

`podman info

  cgroupControllers: []

  conmon:

  cpus: 8

  eventLogger: file

  idMappings:
    gidmap:
    - container_id: 0

    uidmap:

  kernel: 3.10.0-1160.49.1.el7.x86_64

      +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +YAJL
  os: linux
  remoteSocket:
    path: /tmp/podman-run-1002/podman/podman.sock
  security:
    apparmorEnabled: false
    capabilities: CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT

  swapFree: 0

  graphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  imageStore:

  runRoot: /tmp/podman-run-1002/containers

version:
  APIVersion: 3.4.4
  Built: 1640010710
  BuiltTime: Mon Dec 20 22:31:50 2021
  GitCommit: f6526ada1025c2e3f88745ba83b8b461ca659933
  GoVersion: go1.17.5

That is a separate error, please file a new issue.
